svg.text: HAUSA + FULFULDE — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 29D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. HAUSA PROVERB — ON PATIENCE AND TIME —
Hakūri maganin duniyà.
Patience medicine-of world.
"Patience is the medicine of the world." — Core Hausa proverb. Hakūri = patience/endurance.
— II. HAUSA PROVERB — ON KNOWLEDGE —
Ilimi kāyà nà kan kāyà.
Knowledge load NEG on load.
"Knowledge is a load that does not weigh upon other loads." — Hausa saying on the value of education.
— III. HAUSA BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
An yi wannan don ku, ba komi,
daga mutanen da suka tuna gaba.
Passive-made this for you no-cost / from people who COMPL remember forward.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward." (Hausa)
— HAUSA NOTE — “FORWARD” AND “REMEMBER” —
Gaba = forward, ahead, front — and by extension: the future. “Tuna gaba” = remember-forward.
Tuna = to remember, recall (from Arabic dhikr influence). “An yi” = passive construction (impersonal).
SECONDARY — FULFULDE (FULA) — RUNNING TEXT
— I. FULFULDE PROVERB — ON CATTLE AND LIFE —
Naŋga wirwiraa, leydi wella.
Cattle roam-freely earth becomes-good.
"When cattle roam freely, the earth is good." — Fulani pastoral proverb. Freedom and land as linked.
— II. FULFULDE PROVERB — ON MEMORY —
Ko tigiɳaa, ko wollaay.
What is-remembered REL not-forgotten.
"What is remembered is not forgotten." — Fulani oral tradition on the value of memory.
— III. FULFULDE BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Oo ko waraama on, ɗon muuyani,
e nder yimbe jiɗɗi ɗam gaweɗi.
This PASS made for-you freely / in among people who-remembered forward.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (Fulfulde)
— FULFULDE NOTE — “FORWARD / FUTURE” —
Gaweɗi = the future, what lies forward/ahead (from gawo “next, after” + ɗi noun class suffix).
Tigiɳi = to remember, recall (ɳ = palatal flap, distinctive to Fulfulde phonology).
Noun class: gaweɗi carries -ɗi class suffix marking abstract temporal nouns.
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — THE SAHEL CROSSROADS —
Hausa: ~80M speakers (L1+L2). Trade lingua franca of West Africa. Afro-Asiatic family.
Fulfulde: ~40M speakers. Widest-ranging African language geographically. Niger-Congo family.
The Sahel: zone where desert meets savanna — where Hausa merchants and Fulani herders have traded for millennia.
Many Hausa-speakers are multilingual in Fulfulde and vice versa. A natural bilingual community.
PLATE 29D ALT · Hausa / Fulfulde · RUNNING TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
